title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: Running using Vulkan and v2.75 Modders Build. Had to restart Steam after enabling Vulkan within game and restarting the game. Doesn't appear to allow fullscreen with Vulkan, as it's running only in windowed or borderless window, albeit flawlessly.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: Vulkan and DX11 work fine although MP is grayed out due to differences in the files.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes:
Recently did a benchmark on this to compare Vulkan vs Dx11 (DXVK) to see how they perform on "Crazy" settings.

On my machine I got an average of 35 fps using the Vulkan backend.
I got an average of 52 fps using Dx11 (DXVK)

For now I recommend everyone sticks with DX11.

Note: I am not sure how close a comparison this is as Vulkan might actually be competing with the DX12 backend which I assume provides more graphical features than Dx11?
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: The launcher is shotty and untextured, but functional. You'll have to play around a bit with where the buttons are to start the game. In the tutorial I experienced audio issues with the narrator dialogue, but not with the game audio itself. Multiplayer cannot be started because of a file-mismatch in the application. Serviceable, but with the lack of multiplayer, not more then bronze.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: After disabling Esync the game is fully functional with no issues. Vulkan performs better then DXVK and on Windows Vulkan performs better then both DX11+DX12. DXVK has issues with this game on higher resolutions (4k). There are FPS dips into the low 20's during parts of the benchmark. FPS drops by more then half during these dips compared to Windows. Vulkan is butter smooth.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes:
The latest Proton 4.2 build has gotten multiplayer working in this game. It no longer fails due to file validaiton.

If it wasnt for the Disable Esync I think this would deserve platinum now!
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes:
The launcher problem is a hassle. Best advice is to press the superkey to see the launcher then guess based on this where the play button is. A hassle and the only problem that I had. 

Works fine on both DX11 and Vulkan but I got a 40% fps boost using Vulkan over DX11 on 4K / Crazy settings. I have a high end systems but the benchmark indicated that even with a 2080ti that the game was GPU capped. Your mileage may vary.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: I have tested the game after switching form an NVIDIA GPU to the AMD RX 590. The game is playable at low and standard graphics preset at 1080p on my System using Proton 4.2.2. I had to select the Direct x 11 API to make the game work over DXVK. Selecting the Vulkan API in the game settings produces an error when the game is lauched. This seems to be specific to the MESA RADV vulkandrivers, because i now the vulkanrenderer works for linuxusers utilizing an NVIDIA GPU.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: What others have described is exactly what I found, but when swtching from DX11 to Vulkan, then the game would hide the launcher, prior the launcher does show, only "behind" the shadow of the compositor (really strange looking effect). I did have the audio issue others have reported, easily sorted out by installing xact_64 with winetricks, but still first I made sure if the default profile did have the xact override in it with winecfg (actually cd /path/to/507490/pfx; WINEPREFIX=`pwd` /path/to/Proton\ 4.2/dist/bin/wine winecfg), and THEN installed xact with winetricks (cd /path/to/507490/pfx; WINEPREFIX=`pwd`  winetricks xact xact_64) and that was it. I have not still tried MP (I don't even know how to play this SP!!)

title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: On Proton it works out of the box with DXVK (D3D11 to Vulkan translation). Tested with RADV and the new Mesa ACO compiler on Plasma Wayland. BUT: Switching to Vulkan will show an error message about outdated drivers when launching the game, so I will rate this only Silver.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes:
The launcher won't render. It shows in the activities overview, and can be interacted with, but it just displays the same as whatever application was in the background when it started. You can skip the launcher by setting launch options to /nolauncher.

The game won't run in vulkan mode. it just gives an error message about bad drivers.

The sound system is pretty buggy. Certain voice clips (most noticeably in the tutorial scenarios) are randomly replaced by loud static. The game also keeps playing music after being killed with alt+f4 and needs to be stopped via the task manager.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation
